stories.headline: Anyone who doesn’t feel for Palestinians is not human: Egypt coach Hassan
stories.summary: Hossam Hassan uses his FIFA World Cup news conference to raise awareness about the plight of Palestinians in Gaza.

stories.headline: China test-fires long-range missile into Pacific, rattling U.S. allies
stories.summary: The unprecedented test of a nuclear-capable submarine-launched missile highlights Beijing’s growing force in the region.

stories.headline: Euronews explains: The verdict that could end Marine Le Pen – and launch Jordan Bardella
stories.summary: If the Paris Court of Appeal upholds Marine Le Pen's five-year ban from public office, the National Rally figurehead's long-held presidential ambitions would effectively be over.

stories.headline: Oil tanker hit by 'unknown projectile' in Strait of Hormuz, British military says
stories.summary: Iranian state television said a liquified natural gas tanker came under attack Tuesday in the Strait of Hormuz off Oman after ignoring warnings, implying Tehran carried out the assault on a tanker it said was carrying natural gas from Qatar.

stories.headline: Hamas dissolves civilian governing body in Gaza after 20 years
stories.summary: Hamas has announced the dissolution of its civilian governing body in Gaza after nearly 20 years of administration.

stories.headline: Fatal Typhoon Maysak floods burst dam wall in China
stories.summary: At least two have been killed by flooding due to Maysak, while another 480,000 have been evacuated across the southern Guangxi province.

stories.headline: Europe’s Defense Stocks Rally Has Much Riding on NATO Summit
stories.summary: A fledgling rebound in European defense stocks faces a test this week, as investors look to a NATO summit for further clarity on member states’ defense spending plans.

stories.headline: US budget slashing risks losing global scientific edge to China
stories.summary: Experts warn that cuts to US research funding is weakening the country’s scientific and technological capacity.

stories.headline: Tanker on fire off coast of Oman after being hit by projectile
stories.summary: Iran's TV claims the tanker ignored warnings, but no direct responsibility for the attack has been declared.

stories.headline: Cuba sees nationwide power blackout for third time in six months
stories.summary: People in Cuba already faced an ongoing economic and humanitarian crisis, largely due to a US blockade.

stories.headline: Venezuela earthquakes death toll jumps to more than 3,500
stories.summary: Experts warn of an impending health crisis, with thousands sleeping in crowded temporary shelters.

stories.headline: ‘MAGA agenda on display’: FIFA, Trump slammed amid Balogun red card row
stories.summary: Football experts, officials warn of 'dangerous precedent' set by FIFA after U-turn on red card for USA forward Balogun.

stories.headline: Why el-Obeid matters as Sudan’s war enters a new phase
stories.summary: How fighting for el-Obeid could shape both Sudan's war and its humanitarian crisis.

stories.headline: China Buys More US Soybeans as Key Agricultural Trade Ramps Up
stories.summary: China has purchased more soybeans from the US, according to people familiar with the matter, as the key agricultural trade gains momentum following a summit between the leaders of the two nations in May.

stories.headline: Hong Kong Begins Trial Operation of New Gold Clearing System
stories.summary: Hong Kong has launched the trial operation of a new gold clearing system, backed by several major banks, in a key step toward its ambition to become a significant bullion-trading hub with price-setting power.

stories.headline: Wall Street Warms to India’s Markets as Oil Pressures Fade
stories.summary: After spending more than a year on the sidelines, global investors are showing fresh interest in India as sentiment begins to improve across the country’s markets.

stories.headline: Trump’s Tenuous Relationship With NATO Allies
stories.summary: At this year’s NATO summit in Turkey, President Trump is arriving with his own priorities — and, as Tyler Pager, The Times’s White House correspondent, explains, they do not necessarily align with those of other NATO leaders.

stories.headline: Platner’s Democratic Support Evaporates After Sexual Assault Allegation
stories.summary: Graham Platner, the Democratic Senate candidate in Maine, called the allegation false but said he would “reflect” on his political path forward, as many allies rescinded their endorsements.

stories.headline: Top Democrats pull Graham Platner endorsements, call on him to end Maine Senate campaign
stories.summary: The Democrat, who has withstood other scandals since launching his challenge to Sen. Susan Collins (R), is accused of sexual assault by a woman he previously dated.

stories.headline: Australian PM says Chinese missile test could have caused ‘considerable damage’ if weaponised
stories.summary: Solomon Islands prime minister says he doesn’t want to see more countries testing ICBMs in Pacific, adding ‘be our friend but don’t threaten us’ Australian prime minister Anthony Albanese has said China’s weapons test in the Pacific risks fuelling dangerous nuclear proliferation, with the intercontinental ballistic missile (ICBM) fired on Monday capable of causing “considerable damage” if weaponised.International condemnation has grown overnight

stories.headline: Nato braces for difficult summit as Trump puts pressure on spending
stories.summary: Meeting of 32 member states comes at crucial time for alliance after tensions with US over Iran and GreenlandNato leaders will gather in Ankara on Tuesday after a turbulent six months. And as the US continues to pressure its allies to increase defence spending, the other 31 members of the alliance will be hoping to mollify an unpredictable Donald Trump.On Monday, Mark Rutte, Nato’s secretary general, called for the allies to present “clear, concr

stories.headline: China overtakes US in fintech patent filings to seize global lead
Philippine Senate begins Vice President Duterte impeachment trial
Why some African nations are turning down Trump aid money
stories.summary: The Trump administration sees the aid as more transactional - but some say it is not a fair trade.

stories.headline: HSBC pulls back from riskier private credit lending
stories.summary: Europe’s biggest bank told some clients in recent weeks that it would not renew facilities

stories.headline: Samsung Results Trigger Stock Rotation to Less Loved Sectors
stories.summary: Asian tech stocks slumped, as investors used Samsung Electronics Co.’s results to lock in profits on a stellar year-to-date chip rally, rotating into unloved sectors less prone to earnings shocks.

stories.headline: India Property Developer K Raheja Said to Delay $700 Million IPO
stories.summary: K Raheja Corp. Pvt., one of India’s largest real estate developers, has delayed plans for an initial public offering after feedback from investment bankers, according to people familiar with the matter.

stories.headline: Samsung's Record Profit Fails to Impress | The Asia Trade 7/7/2026
stories.summary: "Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Mystery Thai Investor’s Near $1 Billion True Stake Sparks Probe
stories.summary: Thailand’s market regulator is probing the sudden emergence of an individual shareholder who has amassed nearly $1 billion worth of shares in True Corp., making her one of the telecom giant’s largest shareholders.

stories.headline: Japan’s Inpex Signs 15-Year Deal to Buy Persian Gulf LNG
stories.summary: Japan’s state-backed Inpex Corp. signed a 15-year deal with Abu Dhabi National Oil Co. for the supply of liquefied natural gas from its project in the Persian Gulf.

stories.headline: PBOC Unveils New Measures to Cement Hong Kong’s Yuan Hub Status
stories.summary: China’s central bank announced a slew of measures aimed at expanding an investment channel between Hong Kong’s financial market and the mainland, as well as enhancing the city’s role in promoting overseas use of the yuan.

stories.headline: India’s Unloved Heavyweight Stocks May Be Poised for a Rebound
stories.summary: A combination of under-ownership and attractive valuations could draw buyers.

stories.headline: Indian security analysts alarmed by Bangladesh-China port deal
Japan attracts young Indian researchers to collaborate on AI, chemistry
New MUFG president targets ROE on par with JPMorgan
Ukraine warns of interceptor missile shortage as 23 killed in Kyiv region
stories.summary: President Zelensky says Sunday's "massive Russian attack" on Kyiv consisted of 68 missiles and 351 strike drones.

stories.headline: RSF razes multiple villages in Darfur killing dozens as UN warns of rising attacks on children
stories.summary: Sudan’s paramilitary Rapid Support Forces have razed multiple villages in the western regions of the country bordering Chad, killing many and displacing thousands as a separate UN report outlines that over 300 children were killed in their atrocities this year alone.

stories.headline: Ukraine hits vital oil refinery more than 2,500km behind Russian border
stories.summary: While Russia continues to pound Ukraine with missiles and drones, killing at least 19 people and injuring dozens more in Kyiv, Ukraine is striking deep inside Russian territory as part of a campaign to degrade the Kremlin’s war machine.

stories.headline: Watch: Russian attack leaves gaping hole in Kyiv residential building
stories.summary: The BBC's Sarah Rainsford is at the scene of one of the blasts to hit Ukraine's capital overnight.

stories.headline: Russian attack leaves gaping hole in Kyiv residential building
stories.summary: The BBC's Sarah Rainsford is at the scene of one of the blasts to hit Ukraine's capital overnight.

stories.headline: Trump confirms he asked Fifa to review Balogun ban
stories.summary: President Donald Trump confirms he asked Fifa to review United States striker Folarin Balogun's one-match suspension at the World Cup.

stories.headline: Super Typhoon Bavi strikes US Pacific islands with pummeling winds
stories.summary: The storm lashed the Mariana Islands, hitting Rota and prompting emergency warnings on Guam.

stories.headline: France's Marine Le Pen, who brought hard-right National Rally to cusp of electoral success
stories.summary: She has tasted defeat before, but if the appeal court bars her from running in the 2027 presidential election, it could end her political career.

stories.headline: Headteacher among four killed in deadliest Israeli attack on Lebanon since latest ceasefire
stories.summary: Three women and one man were travelling in a car after visiting their family home, Lebanese state media reported.

stories.headline: China tests missile in the Pacific hours after Australia-Fiji alliance signed
stories.summary: The test launch came hours after Australia signed a new defence pact with its Pacific island neighbour Fiji.

stories.headline: Canada to buy 12 hi-tech German submarines after bidding war
stories.summary: TKMS beats South Korean rival to multibillion-dollar contract that will deepen Canada’s Nato tiesCanada has selected a German consortium to build a dozen cutting-edge submarines in one of the country’s largest-ever defence contracts that will further deepen its Nato ties before a crucial summit this week.On Monday the prime minister, Mark Carney, announced the winner of a tightly contested battle for the lucrative government contract to replace i

stories.headline: Typhoon Maysak kills two and forces thousands to evacuate in China
stories.summary: Tropical storm causes extreme flooding in south of the country with heavy rainfall expected in coming daysA tropical storm has killed two people, caused dam breaches and forced tens of thousands to evacuate in southern China.Typhoon Maysak killed two people in Nanning, in China’s southern Guangxi province. Maysak, which lashed Vietnam and China’s southern island province of Hainan over the weekend, will dump the water it sucked up on its way ⁠acr

stories.headline: China tests long-range missile in South Pacific in move Australia condemns as ‘destabilising to region’
stories.summary: Launch comes just hours after Australia and Fiji sign defence agreement as expert says timing not a coincidenceChina has conducted a long-range missile test in the South Pacific just hours after Australia signed a defence agreement with Fiji, sparking condemnation from Canberra and regional leaders.The Australian foreign minister, Penny Wong, said the missile test was “destabilising” to the region, while her New Zealand counterpart, Winston Peter

stories.headline: Starmer to rally European allies at Nato summit amid concerns over US stance
stories.summary: Outgoing PM will stress British commitment to defence spending at meeting in Ankara to counter Trump rebukesKeir Starmer will seek to work with European allies to shore up support for Nato at its summit in Ankara on Tuesday amid concerns that Donald Trump could further destabilise the military alliance with threats over defence spending.Downing Street said the prime minister and other international leaders would be focused on “building a stronger

stories.headline: Space debris on Queensland beach: 'space balls' washed ashore, do not touch
stories.summary: In Australia, authorities warn after several so-called space balls appeared on a beach in Queensland. Anyone who finds one must call emergency services at once and must not touch it.

stories.headline: Jordan Bardella: The 'blank canvas' who could be France's youngest president
stories.summary: Now president of National Rally, Bardella could become the party's candidate for next year's presidential election if Marine Le Pen is barred.

stories.headline: Oil Climbs as Fresh Tanker Strike Highlights Risks Around Hormuz
stories.summary: Oil gained following attacks on vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting continued risks to traffic in the waterway.

stories.headline: Who Might Replace Graham Platner if He Drops Out of the Maine Senate Race?
stories.summary: Graham Platner can be replaced on the ballot if he withdraws in the next week. If he does, Maine Democrats would face an uncertain two-week race to choose a replacement.

stories.headline: Trump Defends Asking FIFA to Review Folarin Balogun’s World Cup Ban
stories.summary: The president said he called the head of FIFA but “didn’t tell him what to do.” The overturning of the ban has roiled soccer and brought renewed scrutiny to the relationship between the two leaders.

stories.headline: After America’s 250th, Trump will test how far he can push NATO allies
stories.summary: As NATO leaders gather in Turkey, they are preparing for another summit shaped as much by managing President Donald Trump as by the alliance's security agenda.

stories.headline: ‘Living like this is agony’: Cuba suffers third nationwide blackout in six months
stories.summary: Impoverished island was already struggling to keep the lights on before the US imposed a blockade in JanuaryCuba on Monday suffered its third nationwide power outage since the start of the year, the state electricity company said.The impoverished island was already struggling to keep the lights on before the US president, Donald Trump, imposed an oil blockade in January, which has depleted the already dwindling supply of fuel for Cuba’s power pla

stories.headline: Watch: Why Australia's PM apologised for 'inappropriate' Kylie comments
stories.summary: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has apologised for remarks he made about popstar Kylie Minogue in a podcast interview last week.

stories.headline: Bangladesh courts China even as ties with India improve
stories.summary: Relations between Dhaka and Delhi, that turned frosty under the interim government, are on the mend.

stories.headline: Samsung’s Record Profit Fails to Impress After AI Chip Rally
stories.summary: Samsung Electronics Co.’s quarterly profit surged 19-fold but failed to impress investors used to eye-catching growth numbers from the suppliers of chips to the global AI boom.

stories.headline: Stock Losses Extend on Chip Selloff, Oil Climbs: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Asian equities fell as renewed selling in technology stocks deepened concerns that the AI-driven rally may have run ahead of itself. Oil rose after a Qatari LNG ship was struck.

stories.headline: South Aegean bids for 2028 World Region of Gastronomy title
stories.summary: Greece’s island cuisine stands out for its quality, nutritional value and, of course, its irresistible flavour.

stories.headline: Iran's supreme leader absent as senior officials attend ayatollah's funeral
stories.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei has not been seen in public since the attack which killed his father at the outset of the US-Israel war with Iran.

stories.headline: Rodríguez defends Venezuela’s emergency earthquake response as number of bodies expected to soar
stories.summary: Interim president says unrest will not break out despite anger at official response to the 24 June disasterVenezuela’s interim president has defended her country’s emergency response to the twin earthquakes that have killed more than 3,000 people, vowing the country would not descend into social unrest.Many Venezuelans have expressed anger at what they see as the US-backed government’s inadequate response to the 24 June disaster before internatio

stories.headline: Marine Le Pen appeal verdict: Why this moment matters for France
stories.summary: The leader of France's National Rally leads the opinion polls ahead of the 2027 presidential election and will now find out if she can stand.

stories.headline: Djokovic breaks Federer's Wimbledon record to reach quarters
stories.summary: Novak Djokovic claims the all-time record for most men's singles match wins at Wimbledon by beating qualifier Roman Safiullin to reach the quarter-finals.

stories.headline: A global hub for fake luxury goods, Vietnam cracks down on its black market
stories.summary: The Trump administration wants Vietnam to stamp out its booming counterfeit industry. Locals are divided.

stories.headline: Ukraine hits major oil terminal in Russia's St Petersburg
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky says the target is key infrastructure "that generates revenue for Russia's war".

stories.headline: ‘The situation is terrible’: aid workers on life in Sudanese city pummelled by drone strikes
stories.summary: El Obeid becomes key battleground in war between Sudan’s armed forces and their paramilitary enemies, the RSFFatima has lost count of the number of drone attacks on the besieged city of El Obeid in Sudan, but said the attacks this past weekend were the most violent so far.The drones hit schools and fuel stations, killing more than 20 people, including students, she said. “Over the past few months, seeing 40 or 45 drones is the norm. You can liter

stories.headline: 'Flamingo Revolution' takes off as thousands demand Albanian PM's resignation
stories.summary: Flamingos have become a national symbol of protest, triggered by opposition to a luxury development.

stories.headline: Keiko Fujimori declared winner of Peru's presidential election weeks after vote
stories.summary: The daughter of disgraced president Alberto Fujimori is declared winner of tight race nearly a month after voting took place.

stories.headline: UK woman, 21, arrested in Thailand after allegedly stabbing boyfriend to death
stories.summary: Officers found body of 34-year-old man in luxury rental home in Pattaya area, local media saysA 21-year-old British woman has been arrested in Thailand after allegedly fatally stabbing her boyfriend, according to local media reports.The Bangkok Post reported that on Thursday morning local time, officers found the body of a 34-year-old man, who operated a cannabis farm, in a luxury rental home in the Pattaya area, a beachside region two hours from

stories.headline: ‘Give him any award, and he’ll come running’: Narendra Modi racks up honours on overseas trips
stories.summary: Indian prime minister has a habit of collecting awards on his travels, some as their first and only recipientAs Narendra Modi touched down in Seychelles over the weekend, the archipelago nation in the Indian Ocean swiftly bestowed one of its “highest” honours upon the Indian prime minister.Modi beamed as he accepted the Guardian of the Blue Horizon award from Patrick Herminie, the Seychelles president, complete with a trophy and certificate. Cont

stories.headline: 'Two weeks after her death I got a call': Gaza patients face agonising delays for evacuation
stories.summary: An estimated 300 Palestinians referred for treatment abroad have died since the ceasefire began, according to Gaza's Hamas-run health ministry.

stories.headline: Watch: BBC at site of deadly Russian attack on Kyiv flats
stories.summary: Sarah Rainsford reports from the completely destroyed nine-storey block of flats in Ukraine's capital.

stories.headline: Aunt of Venezuelan boy pulled from rubble tells BBC she will give him 'mother's warmth'
stories.summary: Kleiber Moran, 2, was rescued on Wednesday, six days after last week's deadly earthquakes.

stories.headline: Did this AI anti-drug video make drugs look appealing?
stories.summary: Hong Kong’s Correctional Services Department removed an AI-generated K-pop video, after facing backlash that it made substances look too appealing.

stories.headline: The US deported them to Venezuela - hours later earthquakes struck
stories.summary: They landed on Flight 164 just before the twin quakes. Family members are desperately searching for them.

stories.headline: Why the expected fight over the North American trade deal never kicked off
stories.summary: The US has confirmed it will not extend the agreement for another 16 years, but stopped short of more dramatic action.

stories.headline: Syria's president names final 70 lawmakers to new post-Assad parliament
stories.summary: Fifteen women are appointed, after only six female candidates were selected by regional electoral colleges last October.

stories.headline: US blocks long-term renewal of North American trade deal
stories.summary: US blocks 16-year North America trade deal renewal, triggering annual rolling reviews

stories.headline: Families search for earthquake survivors in one of Venezuela's hardest hit areas
stories.summary: BBC correspondent Will Grant reports from Caraballeda, La Guaira, where locals are still desperately searching for family members in the rubble.

stories.headline: Angry Venezuelans accuse government of negligence over earthquake response
stories.summary: People in areas devastated by twin earthquakes say they need more support from the government.
